text.p: Kaa Sa,

Were you asking for permission to quote me in your word bank? Here you have it. I hold no patent for most of the words I use. But in case of litigation, you are on your own. Words here are like the free air, enjoy the much you care to. I am also speaking onbehalf of my good friends.

Did you get the point I tried to make last night? Sound reasoning tells me to chose death if it is your friend that shares life. But the other side of me cries against looking the other way when I am sure that that would mean some loss to him. , when will this battle, this crisis of belief, be over?
How is ? One good thing is that I can boldly tell our people na nwa nnem aga-ala Ala Bekee. You are there now. Enjoy yourself.
His grace will not allow you to fail
Boy,
Your mail only made me remeber how much of me you don't know. I am a complete home boy. That is why I thought that America noo n'uzo Europe? Two of them owugh Ala Bekee?
My humble idea was that your Daily Guide would meet you with speed if given to the s. Sorry.
School. I won't call it biting more than one could chew. God is helping us. The trouble is money and that I know you don't have now.

The prospect is much, if only we could get the means to prepare for September, That is just what we are struggling to do. The spiritual battle invoved in it can mait for another mail. Some people think that education is theirtuff. They are ready to fight you till you forget your name.
It has not been easy.
Kaa Sa,
How is it withyou and all this long silence? I trust you are keeping on. You must have received the Daily Guide by now and stopped taking expired drug. .

How are you managing financially? Please talk so that my wife will know that you are alive. She is so concerned about you that I am getting jealous, not when you refused to contribute money in paying her bride price. Please talk.
We are rest assured that He who has led you this far will not abandon you half way. Your troubles will be doubly rewarded.
May we hear from you.
You are welcome back!
I have gone to my mail but could not see the mail you mentioned in your text. Wherever it is, be assured that yiou are not forgotten, nor ever could be.
The pressure of living often hits harder than ne repares for.
The Day-Care has taken its toll. It leaves nothing to feed those I have to care for at home.Lagos State keeps raising the bar. I guess we need prayers more than before.
Thank God for my wife's determination. I envy her courage.
How is your end? Please keep on keeping on as the dawn is nearer than when you started. You shall definitely overcome.
We are here to celebrate you.
Let me hear from you, please.
The s left here last week Monday. We enjoyed their visit, only it was too short.
God bless you.Kaa.
Mazi,
Kosi rara. Kama ima na onye ara si onabu ya choo ikwu ozo, ozo abata ya n'onu. Ma Jehova na adi ndu. Egwu adighi at all.

We hope the primary school will take off this September. That is keeping us on our toes. Our little strenght is long spent and we are counting on nothing other than His grace. That one, you taught us, never fails. That is our only hope. If it fails, be sure we shall fail, otherwise, no shaking.

We are no longer afraid of owing, if only we see where to borrow. We are a few hundreds of thousands from paying up for the accommodation, then furnishing the place to make it ready for September. Of course, there will be Holiday caoching there, to sensitize the area. Your part is to keep praying for us. After all, you talked me into agreeing with my wife to begin.
Now, I am sure that you will survive, having come this far. Kama, mee kam mara otu gi na project si adoga.
Jisieike. Onye mmeri ka i bu.

Thanks for your mail, a succure to my thirsty soul. I was just beginning to wonder that your lips were too tight, not minding the state of your purse which you already mentioned. Frankly, I was beginning to think you had started avoiding the rouge brother, if at all he ever was one. I feared you might not wish him to corrupt you with the wicked virus. Sure, I am alive, an assurance that there is hope of survival. One day, we shall tell the story of how we overcome; assuming we understand it enough to tell the story.
How are you? Believe God, it is well. It is not over yet.
